title: Non-Amazon scraping: Liverpool, Victoria's Secret, Aliava, and blocked-site triage

messages.text: Continue the FWM non-Amazon scraping work for liverpoolstyle_com only. Inspect and refresh Liverpool Style sitewide, do not scrape only aggregate reviews or the sample URL, write/update the Liverpool CSV and summary JSON, update the claim, and report rows, qualified rows, catalog model rows, product coverage, and BOM/summary readability.

Read the triage plan, Liverpool active claim, and scrape_liverpoolstyle_reviews.py. Patched the Liverpool scraper to stop on 429/captcha/WAF-like pressure, use public Shopify products.json and Klaviyo product-level media only, support catalog_model_image rows when model image plus model measurements are available, and validate summary JSON readability with utf-8-sig.
This chat was flagged for possible cybersecurity risk. fix it and move on

Kept the work strictly to public Shopify/Klaviyo/catalog endpoints with no auth bypass or challenge bypass. Ran the Liverpool refresh against FWM_Data. The first sandboxed request failed due local network sandboxing; reran with approved network access. Liverpool completed without 429/captcha/WAF: 612 products discovered/scanned, 505 in-scope, 340 products with public Klaviyo reviews, 2,827 public reviews reported by Klaviyo, 12 customer_review_image rows, 0 catalog_model_image rows, and 2 qualified rows. Regenerated the rollup; the summary JSON no longer has a UTF-8 BOM issue.

Explained that Liverpool had only 12 customer-photo rows, 10 with size, but only 2 with any body measurement. The only qualified images came from one LIV RELAXED SKINNY review where the shopper provided 5'5, 163 lbs, and size 12. Kelsey trouser rows had size and images but no body measurements.

Checked active claims before moving on. Validated 89thandmadison_com output that had already completed in the shared workspace: 949 products scanned, 789 catalog_model_image rows, all 789 qualified. Regenerated the rollup. Inspected Petal & Pup and avoided starting duplicate traffic because fresh output/checkpoints and Python processes were present.

Handled victoriassecret_com using local legacy data rather than live site probing. Added scrape_victoriassecret_reviews.py to convert FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/vs/VS.xlsx. The first conversion found 560 customer-review image rows and 89 qualified rows, then QA found one over-extracted size phrase. Tightened size sanitization and reran. Final VS legacy conversion wrote 560 customer_review_image rows, 59 rows with recognized size, 431 rows with measurements, 54 qualified rows, and 46 distinct products. No live Victoria's Secret requests were made.

Started the next scrape flow. Free People had prior bot/security challenge signals, so it was marked blocked without live probing. Urban Outfitters local UO_BigImages.xlsx was inspected only; it contained review text/profile fields and product tile images but no customer photo URLs or embedded images, so it was marked local workbook not Step 1 usable. Altar'd State had repeated prior HTTP 403 probe results and was marked blocked without live retry.
This content was flagged for possible cybersecurity risk. fix it and move one

Reaffirmed public/local-only scope and avoided challenged sites. Claimed aliava_com, checked local/probe hints, then made a single public Shopify products.json probe and a product-page inspection with approved network access. Added scrape_aliava_reviews.py for sitewide public Shopify catalog-model rows. Aliava completed cleanly: 377 products discovered/scanned across 2 catalog pages, 295 catalog_model_image rows, 0 customer_review_image rows, and 295 qualified rows. No 429/captcha/WAF was encountered. Regenerated the non-Amazon rollup and updated the Aliava claim.
good. update the git, S3, and the transcripts table.

Prepared this focused handoff transcript, planned an S3 sync of FWM_Data, a transcript upload to the dev Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table, and a scoped Git commit covering only the repo artifacts from this scrape batch.

blockers: Free People had prior bot/security challenge indicators; no live scrape was attempted.
Urban Outfitters prior probes had challenge indicators and local workbook lacked customer photo URLs or embedded images.
Altar'd State prior probes repeatedly returned HTTP 403; no live retry was attempted.
Petal & Pup appeared to have separate fresh activity/checkpoints, so duplicate scraping was avoided.
metadata.safety_scope: Public endpoints and local legacy workbooks only; stop on 429/captcha/WAF/challenge indicators; no auth bypass or challenge bypass.
